text.title: design-docs-big-tech Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for design-docs-big-tech as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using the design doc as a spec — conflating "why/how" with "what".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Design doc answers HOW; spec answers WHAT. Mixing them produces a document that does neither well and forces readers to untangle intent from implementation.

failure-mode.description: No alternatives section — only the chosen solution is documented.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reviewers question judgment when they cannot see what was rejected. Future engineers re-litigate the decision because the reasoning is invisible.

failure-mode.description: Writing the design doc after implementation begins.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Defeats the primary purpose. Architectural issues caught post-implementation cost orders of magnitude more to fix than issues caught at the design stage.

failure-mode.description: Too many approvers — more than 10 participants.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Consensus gridlock; the review process takes longer than the implementation. Google and Amazon both observe this threshold empirically.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without the baseline, reviewers always ask "why not just leave it as is?" Include it to pre-empt the question and force the author to justify the build decision.
